# Index

*Last updated: 2026-05-04 (5-paper HD 189733 b + TLS-foundations batch). Maintained by the LLM — do not edit by hand except to flag errors.*



## Targets (87)

- [[HD-189733b]] — pre-JWST hot Jupiter archetype (1.15 MJ, 1.16 RJ, P = 2.22 d) around active K1V dwarf HD 189733; panchromatic 0.3–24 μm dataset; **dust-haze (Pont 2008/2013) vs. unocculted-starspot TLS (McCullough 2014) tension** is unresolved · 3 papers
- [[HD-189733]] — active K1V dwarf hosting the pre-JWST hot-Jupiter archetype HD 189733 b; ~12 d rotation; ~few-percent photometric variability · 4 papers
- [[HD3167b]] — USP super-Earth (1.60 R⊕, Teq ≈ 1786 K) around K dwarf HD 3167; MIRI/LRS eclipse ~5σ below bare-rock; evidence for atmosphere or high-albedo surface · 1 paper
- [[HD3167]] — K0 dwarf hosting the multi-planet system including the USP lava world HD 3167 b · 1 paper
- [[LHS1140c]] — rocky super-Earth in the LHS 1140 system; MIRI F1500W eclipse rules out atmosphere; consistent with low-albedo bare rock · 1 paper
- [[LHS1140]] — M dwarf hosting LHS 1140 b and c; multi-planet system · 1 paper
- [[LHS1478b]] — rocky planet around M-dwarf LHS 1478; MIRI F1500W eclipse rules out dark bare rock at ~2σ; CO₂-bearing atmosphere or high-albedo surface · 1 paper
- [[LHS1478]] — M dwarf hosting LHS 1478 b · 1 paper
- [[TOI-1468b]] — rocky planet around M dwarf TOI-1468; MIRI F1500W eclipse anomalously deep (T_day ≈ 964 K); origin unexplained · 1 paper
- [[TOI-1468]] — M dwarf hosting TOI-1468 b · 1 paper
- [[WASP-17b]] — inflated retrograde hot Jupiter (0.4 MJ, 1.93 RJ, Teq ≈ 1700 K) around F dwarf WASP-17; JWST DREAMS suite covers dayside (NIRISS) + nightside (NIRSpec); supersolar dayside H₂O; > 5σ limb asymmetry · 3 papers
- [[WASP-17]] — F6 dwarf hosting WASP-17 b; first F-dwarf host in the wiki · 3 papers
- [[WASP-39b]] — inflated hot Jupiter (0.28 MJ, 1.27 RJ, Teq ≈ 1166 K) around G dwarf WASP-39; JWST ERS benchmark target; rich multi-species atmosphere; > 5σ limb asymmetry; methodology benchmark target · 3 papers
- [[WASP-39]] — solar-type G dwarf hosting the hot Jupiter WASP-39 b; JWST ERS benchmark host · 3 papers
- [[WASP-80b]] — warm gas giant (0.55 MJ, 0.95 RJ, Teq ≈ 825 K) around K/early-M dwarf; H₂O, CH₄, CO, CO₂ detected; first wiki target with joint interior–atmosphere retrieval · 2 papers
- [[WASP-80]] — late-K / early-M dwarf hosting the warm gas giant WASP-80 b · 2 papers
- [[WASP-96b]] — inflated hot Saturn (0.48 MJ, 1.20 RJ, Teq ≈ 1300 K) around late-G dwarf; H₂O, CO₂, Na detected; tentative SO₂ on the photochemical shoreline · 2 papers
- [[WASP-96]] — late-G dwarf hosting the hot Saturn WASP-96 b · 2 papers
- [[WASP-107b]] — warm super-puff (0.10 MJ, 0.94 RJ, Teq ≈ 770 K) around K dwarf; first limb-resolved chemical heterogeneity (5× SO₂, 40× CO₂, morning-only cloud) · 2 papers
- [[WASP-107]] — K6V dwarf hosting the warm super-puff WASP-107 b · 2 papers
- [[HAT-P-18b]] — warm Saturn (0.20 MJ, 1.00 RJ, Teq ≈ 848 K) around K dwarf HAT-P-18; both limbs muted in the limb-asymmetry sample · 1 paper
- [[HAT-P-18]] — K2V dwarf hosting HAT-P-18 b · 1 paper
- [[WASP-166b]] — inflated low-mass hot Saturn (0.10 MJ, 0.63 RJ, Teq ≈ 1272 K) around F9V; limb-asymmetry inconclusive · 1 paper
- [[WASP-166]] — F9V dwarf hosting WASP-166 b · 1 paper
- [[WASP-52b]] — inflated hot Jupiter (0.46 MJ, 1.27 RJ, Teq ≈ 1301 K) around active K dwarf; only planet in limb-asymmetry sample with negative δA_H · 1 paper
- [[WASP-52]] — K2V dwarf hosting WASP-52 b; magnetically active · 1 paper
- [[WASP-94Ab]] — inflated hot Jupiter (0.50 MJ, 1.58 RJ, Teq ≈ 1543 K) around F8 binary primary; **strongest limb-asymmetry signal** in the JWST NIRISS sample (δA_H = 2.83 H); LSM normalization reference · 1 paper
- [[WASP-94A]] — F8 dwarf, primary of the WASP-94 visual binary, hosting WASP-94 Ab · 1 paper
- [[GJ1132b]] (hub) — rocky super-Earth on 1.63-day orbit around M4.5 dwarf GJ 1132; current working conclusion is "bare rock" · 4 papers
- [[GJ3473b]] — highly-irradiated rocky super-Earth (1.26 R⊕, Teq ≈ 773 K) around mid-M dwarf; MIRI F1500W R = 0.82 ± 0.12; bare-rock and atmosphere both fit; thick CO₂ excluded · 1 paper
- [[GJ3473]] — mid-M dwarf hosting GJ 3473 b · 1 paper
- [[HAT-P-12b]] — warm low-density sub-Saturn (0.21 MJ, 0.95 RJ, Teq ≈ 960 K) around K4/K5V star; H₂O, CO₂, CO, H₂S detected via panchromatic JWST + HST · 1 paper
- [[HAT-P-12]] — K4/K5V dwarf hosting HAT-P-12 b · 1 paper
- [[HD80606b]] — highly eccentric hot Jupiter (4.16 MJ, 1.03 RJ, e=0.93) around G5V dwarf HD 80606; first wiki target with phase-resolved seasonal atmospheric variability (CH₄, H₂O, CO₂ post-periapse) · 1 paper
- [[HD80606]] — bright Sun-like G5V (V≈9, J=7.7) hosting the highly eccentric hot Jupiter HD 80606 b · 1 paper
- [[HAT-P-26b]] — warm Neptune-mass exoplanet (18.6 M⊕, 6.33 R⊕, T_eq ≈ 1000 K) around K-dwarf HAT-P-26; first SO₂ detection in a Neptune-mass planet · 1 paper
- [[HAT-P-26]] — K-dwarf (T_eff ≈ 5079 K) hosting the warm Neptune-mass HAT-P-26 b · 1 paper
- [[HATS-75b]] — Saturn-mass gas giant (0.49 MJ, 0.88 RJ, P = 2.79 d) around early-M dwarf; first wiki target in the GEMS class; sub-solar atmospheric metallicity in TLS framework · 1 paper
- [[HATS-75]] — early-M dwarf (T_eff ≈ 3790 K) hosting HATS-75 b; active host with spot-crossings · 1 paper
- [[HD-209458b]] — first transiting exoplanet (1999); inflated hot Jupiter; theoretical archetype for transmission spectroscopy; first exoplanet atmosphere ever detected (Na I, Charbonneau 2002, not yet ingested); first wiki JWST-era characterization (Verma 2025) · 3 papers
- [[HD-209458]] — G0V star hosting HD 209458 b; bright host (V ≈ 7.65) that enabled the first transit detection · 3 papers
- [[V1298Tau-b]] — young (10–30 Myr) sub-Neptune progenitor (12 M⊕, 9.85 R⊕, Teq ≈ 670 K) around K-type T Tauri host; ~0.6× solar metallicity; subsolar C/O ≈ 0.22; CH₄ thermometer reveals high T_int ≈ 500 K inconsistent with evolutionary models; mass measured from atmospheric scale height retiring RV upper limit · 1 paper
- [[V1298Tau]] — weak-lined K-type T Tauri star (~10–30 Myr, T_eff ≈ 5050 K, M⋆ ≈ 1.18 M☉) in Taurus-Auriga; hosts four planets including the young sub-Neptune progenitor V1298 Tau b · 1 paper
- [[KELT-7b]] — aligned hot Jupiter (1.39 MJ, 1.60 RJ, T_eq ≈ 2048 K) around F2 dwarf KELT-7; third BOWIE-ALIGN target; weak features only — high cloud deck or low metallicity · 1 paper
- [[KELT-7]] — F2 dwarf (T_eff ≈ 6768 K) hosting the aligned hot Jupiter KELT-7 b · 1 paper
- [[K2-18b]] — habitable-zone sub-Neptune (2.61 R⊕, T_eq ≈ 255 K) around M3 dwarf; Hycean-candidate with disputed JWST DMS / CO₂ claims; comparative reference in Roy 2025 · 1 paper
- [[K2-18]] — M3V dwarf hosting the Hycean-candidate K2-18 b · 1 paper
- [[LP-791-18c]] — temperate sub-Neptune (2.44 R⊕, T_eq ≈ 355 K) around M6 dwarf; strong haze + CH₄ detected; **no CO₂** — diversity contrast with K2-18 b/TOI-270 d · 1 paper
- [[LP-791-18]] — M6 dwarf hosting the temperate sub-Neptune LP 791-18 c · 1 paper
- [[TOI-270b]] — warm super-Earth (1.306 R⊕, 1.48 M⊕, Teq ≈ 569 K) in the TOI-270 system; ρ = 3.7 g/cm³ (4.4σ underdense vs. Earth-like); tentative H₂O feature; TLS ruled out via simultaneous TOI-270 d transit · 1 paper
- [[TOI-270d]] — temperate sub-Neptune in the TOI-270 multi-planet system; clear CH₄ + CO₂; comparative reference · 2 papers
- [[TOI-270]] — M3V dwarf hosting the TOI-270 multi-planet system including the super-Earth TOI-270 b and the sub-Neptune TOI-270 d · 2 papers
- [[GJ1132]] — nearby M4.5 dwarf hosting GJ 1132 b · 4 papers
- [[K2-22b]] — disintegrating ultra-short-period rocky planet (P=9.1 hr, Teq≈3830 K) actively losing mass as dust and gas tails; tentative NO gas and iron-rich silicate dust detected · 1 paper
- [[K2-22]] — K-type dwarf hosting the disintegrating rocky planet K2-22 b · 1 paper
- [[GJ357b]] — super-Earth (Teq≈525 K) around the exceptionally inactive M2.5V dwarf GJ 357; featureless NIRISS SOSS + NIRSpec G395H spectra (0.85–5.14 μm); high-metallicity secondary atmosphere not yet excluded · 2 papers
- [[GJ357]] — nearby inactive M2.5V dwarf hosting the transiting super-Earth GJ 357 b; XUV sub-threshold for rapid escape since ~1 Gyr · 2 papers
- [[GJ486b]] — hot rocky planet (Teq~696 K) around M3.5 dwarf GJ 486; transmission/emission pair resolved in favor of starspot contamination + airless body · 2 papers
- [[GJ486]] — bright nearby M3.5 dwarf hosting GJ 486 b · 2 papers
- [[GJ3929b]] — near-Earth-sized rocky planet (1.09 R⊕, Teq ≈ 568 K) around M3.5V GJ 3929; MIRI F1500W eclipse photometry consistent with bare rock; CO₂ > 100 mbar excluded · 1 paper
- [[GJ3929]] — nearby M3.5V dwarf hosting transiting rocky planet GJ 3929 b and two additional planet candidates · 1 paper
- [[L168-9b]] — hot super-Earth (Teq=981 K) around the bright M1V dwarf L 168-9; featureless 3–12 μm spectrum · 1 paper
- [[L168-9]] — M1V dwarf hosting L 168-9 b (TOI-134) · 1 paper
- [[L98-59c]] — warm super-Earth around the bright M3V dwarf L 98-59; featureless JWST COMPASS spectrum · 1 paper
- [[L98-59d]] — warm super-Earth around L 98-59, water-world candidate; two independent JWST analyses hint at H₂S/SO₂ atmosphere · 2 papers
- [[L98-59]] — bright nearby M3V dwarf hosting three transiting rocky planets · 3 papers
- [[LHS475b]] — Earth-sized rocky planet on 2.03-day orbit around M3.5V LHS 475; featureless, H₂ ruled out at >10σ · 1 paper
- [[LHS475]] — nearby low-activity M3.5V dwarf hosting LHS 475 b · 1 paper
- [[LTT1445Ab]] — rocky planet in the closest transiting-exoplanet system (6.86 pc); MIRI emission rules out thick atmospheres · 1 paper
- [[LTT1445A]] — primary M-dwarf of the nearby triple system LTT 1445 · 1 paper
- [[TOI-561b]] — dense USP super-Earth (1.45 R⊕, Teq ≈ 2300 K) around ancient K-dwarf (~10 Gyr); G395H secondary eclipses indicate T_day far below bare-rock prediction, consistent with thick volatile-rich atmosphere; challenges cosmic shoreline · 1 paper
- [[TOI-561]] — ancient (~10 Gyr) metal-poor thick-disk K-dwarf hosting TOI-561 b · 1 paper
- [[TOI-776b]] — warm super-Earth (Teq~520 K) around the bright M-dwarf TOI-776; inner of a multi-planet system · 1 paper
- [[TOI-776c]] — warm sub-Neptune (Teq≈420 K, 2.02 R⊕) around M1V TOI-776; water-world candidate; featureless COMPASS NIRSpec G395H spectrum · 1 paper
- [[TOI-776]] — bright M1V dwarf (LP 961-53) hosting super-Earth TOI-776 b and water-world candidate TOI-776 c · 2 papers
- [[TOI-836b]] — super-Earth around the bright K-dwarf TOI-836; flat spectrum, no H₂-dominated atmosphere · 2 papers
- [[TOI-836c]] — sub-Neptune sibling to TOI-836 b; flat NIRSpec G395H from the COMPASS uniform reanalysis; hazy-sub-Neptune candidate analogous to GJ 1214 b · 1 paper
- [[TOI-836]] — bright K-dwarf (HIP 73427) hosting the multi-planet TOI-836 system · 1 paper
- [[TOI-1685b]] — hot USP super-Earth (1.468 R⊕, Teq ≈ 1062 K) around M2.5V host; five G395H transits flat; MMW ≳ 10 at ~5σ; bare rock · 1 paper
- [[TOI-1685]] — M2.5V dwarf hosting the ultra-short-period super-Earth TOI-1685 b · 1 paper
- [[TRAPPIST-1b]] — innermost of the seven TRAPPIST-1 planets; near-airless reference body in back-to-back-transit corrections; NIRISS/SOSS dominated by stellar contamination; MIRI F1500W bare rock · 3 papers
- [[TRAPPIST-1c]] — second of the seven TRAPPIST-1 planets; corrected for stellar contamination via TRAPPIST-1 b in 2025; MIRI F1500W reanalysis cooler than original — atmosphere/high-albedo case strengthened · 2 papers
- [[TRAPPIST-1d]] — habitable-zone rocky sub-Earth (0.804 R⊕, Teq ≈ 282 K); H₂ excluded >3σ; cloud-free terrestrial analogs excluded >95%; N₂-rich consistent · 1 paper
- [[TRAPPIST-1e]] — habitable-zone rocky planet (0.920 R⊕, Teq ≈ 251 K); H₂-rich and thick CO₂ ruled out; GP stellar contamination marginalization; N₂-rich atmosphere not excluded · 2 papers
- [[TRAPPIST-1f]] — habitable-zone rocky planet (1.045 R⊕, Teq ≈ 220 K); JWST PRISM data from GTO 1201 reanalyzed for flares (Howard 2025); no atmospheric claim yet · 1 paper
- [[TRAPPIST-1g]] — outer habitable-zone rocky planet (1.129 R⊕, Teq ≈ 197 K); JWST NIRISS/SOSS data from GO 2589 reanalyzed for flares (Howard 2025); no atmospheric claim yet · 1 paper
- [[TRAPPIST-1]] (hub) — M8V ultracool dwarf hosting seven transiting terrestrial planets; highly heterogeneous photosphere; three contamination-mitigation strategies documented across six papers · 6 papers

## Instruments (13)

- [[CHEOPS]] — ESA optical photometric space telescope (330–1100 nm); used for simultaneous transit monitoring alongside JWST · 1 paper
- [[HST-ACS]] — Hubble ACS G800L grism (550–1050 nm); pre-JWST optical transmission for HD 189733 b · 2 papers
- [[HST-NICMOS]] — Hubble NICMOS (1.0–2.5 μm); G206 grism + F166N/F187N photometry; pre-JWST · 1 paper
- [[HST-STIS]] — Hubble Space Telescope Imaging Spectrograph (G430L+G750L, 0.29–1.02 μm); HST optical transmission workhorse · 3 papers
- [[HST-WFC3]] — Hubble WFC3 G141/G102 grism; pre-JWST workhorse for 1.1–1.7 μm transmission spectroscopy · 6 papers
- [[NIRPS]] — ESO 3.6 m NIR (0.98–1.80 μm, R ~ 70 000) high-resolution spectrograph; used for M-dwarf host-star abundances feeding JWST interior priors · 1 paper
- [[Spitzer-IRAC]] — Spitzer IRAC 3.6/4.5/5.8/8 μm photometry; pre-JWST hot-Jupiter mid-IR workhorse · 1 paper
- [[Spitzer-MIPS]] — Spitzer MIPS 24 μm photometry; long-wavelength anchor for pre-JWST panchromatic spectra · 1 paper
- [[JWST-MIRI]] (hub) — JWST mid-infrared instrument (5–28 μm); LRS slitless mode for small-planet time-series; also F1500W photometric imaging for eclipse temperature measurements · 13 papers
- [[JWST-NIRCam]] — JWST near-infrared camera with grism time-series modes (F322W2, F444W); valuable for hot/warm-Jupiter transmission and emission · 3 papers
- [[JWST-NIRISS]] — JWST near-infrared imager and slitless spectrograph (0.6–2.8 μm); SOSS mode for transit and eclipse spectroscopy; blue complement to NIRSpec · 8 papers
- [[JWST-NIRSpec]] (hub) — JWST near-infrared spectrograph (0.6–5.3 μm); G395H/G395M BOTS modes for transit spectroscopy and PRISM mode for ultracool-dwarf hosts · 28 papers
- [[VLT-FORS2]] — ESO VLT FORS2 (0.36–1.0 μm); ground-based optical workhorse for transmission spectroscopy in the pre-JWST era · 1 paper

## Methods (10)

- [[atmospheric-retrievals]] (hub) — Bayesian inversion of transmission/emission spectra for composition, T–P, and cloud parameters · 23 papers
- [[back-to-back-transit-correction]] — empirical, model-independent stellar-contamination correction using a near-airless reference planet in the same system · 1 paper
- [[flare-mitigation]] — empirical and physics-based subtraction of stellar flares from JWST NIR transmission spectra · 2 papers
- [[information-content-analysis]] — Bayesian leave-one-out instrument-attribution; Δln Z per instrument combination · 1 paper
- [[joint-interior-atmosphere-retrieval]] — single Bayesian likelihood combining interior-structure and atmospheric forward models with bulk observables · 1 paper
- [[multi-visit-stacking]] (hub) — weighted coadding of multiple transit visits plus leave-one-out diagnostics; a rocky-planet detection discriminator · 16 papers
- [[planetary-infrared-excess]] — emission-spectrum extraction sidestepping stellar-model imprecision (notably the iESR variant for transiting planets) · 1 paper
- [[secondary-eclipse-spectroscopy]] — thermal-emission measurement via eclipse-depth spectroscopy; constrains atmospheric retention via the T_day/T_max ratio · 10 papers
- [[stellar-contamination-modeling]] (hub) — retrieval/forward modeling of the transit light source effect from unocculted spots/faculae · 14 papers
- [[transmission-spectroscopy]] (hub) — measurement of Rp(λ) via wavelength-resolved transit depths · 31 papers

## Species (15)

- [[CH4]] (hub) · [[CO2]] (hub) · [[H2O]] (hub) · [[SO2]] (hub) — each in 4+ papers
- [[CS2]] — 1 paper (carbon disulfide; tentative on TOI-270 d, degenerate with CH₃Cl/CH₃F/H₂CS at JWST/NIRSpec resolution)
- [[DMS]] — 1 paper (dimethyl sulfide; biosignature candidate; **retracted** on K2-18 b under comprehensive retrievals)
- [[NH3]] — 1 paper (weak preference on TOI-270 d; not detected on KELT-7 b or HAT-P-26 b)
- [[CO]] — 4 papers (detected in WASP-39 b; HAT-P-12 b; HATS-75 b; V1298 Tau b; CO 2.3 μm overtone predicted in Brown 2001)
- [[FeH]] — 1 paper (tentatively detected in WASP-17 b dayside, Gressier 2025)
- [[H2S]] — 6 papers (tentative on L 98-59 d; first firm detection on HAT-P-12 b; tentative ~2σ on Neptune-mass HAT-P-26 b; non-tentative on WASP-39 b in NEXOTRANS)
- [[He]] — 1 paper (predicted by Seager & Sasselov 2000; metastable 1083 nm)
- [[K]] — 4 papers (detected in WASP-39 b; HD 209458 b in Verma 2025 STIS+WFC3+NIRCam reanalysis; foundational predictions in 2000/2001 theory papers)
- [[N2O]] — 1 paper
- [[Na]] — 4 papers (detected in WASP-39 b, WASP-96 b, HD 209458 b; foundational prediction in 2000/2001 theory papers)
- [[NO]] — 1 paper (tentatively detected in K2-22 b escaping gas, Tusay 2025)

## Concepts (9)

- [[aerosols]] — clouds, hazes, condensate grains, photochemical soots; continuum opacity in transmission spectra; canonical case is HD 189733 b's panchromatic Rayleigh slope · 5 papers
- [[cosmic-shoreline]] — Zahnle & Catling 2017 boundary between atmosphere-retaining and atmosphere-less rocky planets · 10 papers
- [[disintegrating-planet]] — class of USP rocky planets undergoing active mass loss; dust/gas tails create variable, wavelength-dependent transit signatures probing interior composition · 1 paper
- [[GEMS]] — Giant Exoplanets around M-dwarf Stars; class combining hot/warm-Jupiter chemistry with M-dwarf stellar contamination · 1 paper
- [[limb-asymmetry]] — morning–evening terminator transmission contrast on tidally locked planets; LSM and asymmetry horizon; chemical heterogeneity (5× SO₂, 40× CO₂, uniform H₂O on WASP-107 b) · 2 papers
- [[SO2-photochemical-shoreline]] — proposed (Teq, metallicity) boundary above which photochemical SO₂ becomes detectable; first limb-resolved validation on WASP-107 b · 2 papers
- [[stellar-contamination]] (hub) — the transit light source effect; a dominant systematic for M-dwarf planets and now also implicated in the HD 189733 b panchromatic-slope reinterpretation · 19 papers
- [[sub-neptune-classification]] — two-axis (T_eq × H₂ content) classification scheme for volatile-rich sub-Neptunes (Madhusudhan 2025): ice / hycean / mini-Neptune / gas dwarf / supercritical mini-Neptune / steam world; first young-progenitor anchor (V1298 Tau b) · 2 papers
- [[transmission-spectrum]] — the wavelength-dependent transit depth observable · 20 papers

## Codes (62)

- [[BeAR]] — Bayesian atmospheric retrieval framework (renamed HELIOS-R2; Kitzmann et al. 2020); supports native-resolution retrieval with LSF convolution · 1 paper
- [[HELIOS-K]] — GPU-accelerated opacity calculation code (Grimm & Heng 2015); backend for BeAR · 1 paper
- [[Ahsoka]] — composite NIRISS/SOSS reduction pipeline (Louie et al. submitted; combines `jwst calibration`, supreme-SPOON, nirHiss, Eureka!) · 1 paper
- [[ARCiS]] — Bayesian transmission/emission retrieval framework (Min et al. 2020); ExoMIRI consortium standard · 2 papers
- [[ATMO]] — 1D self-consistent radiative-convective grid for hot-Jupiter forward models (Tremblin et al.; Goyal et al.) · 3 papers
- [[Aurora]] — Bayesian transmission-retrieval code with limb-asymmetry support (Welbanks & Madhusudhan 2021) · 3 papers
- [[CASCADe]] — JWST/HST time-series pipeline (Carone et al. 2021); ExoMIRI consortium standard · 2 papers
- [[Catwoman]] — asymmetric two-semicircle transit-light-curve model (Jones & Espinoza 2020); extracts limb-resolved transmission spectra · 2 papers
- [[CHIMERA]] — thermochemical-equilibrium forward-modeling code · 7 papers
- [[easyCHEM]] — chemical equilibrium code (Mollière et al. 2017); commonly used as the equilibrium-chemistry module for petitRADTRANS · 2 papers
- [[Erebus]] — JWST MIRI eclipse-photometry pipeline implementing FN-PCA detrending (Connors et al. 2025) · 1 paper
- [[ExoTEP]] — JWST/HST light-curve fitting framework (Benneke et al.); often paired with Eureka! · 2 papers
- [[FastChem]] — fast gas-phase chemical equilibrium code for exoplanet atmospheres (Stock et al. 2018, 2022) · 4 papers
- [[fleck]] — fast forward-modeling of unocculted starspots/faculae on rotating stars (Morris 2020) · 1 paper
- [[GASTLI]] — open-source gas-giant interior structure code (Acuña et al. 2021–2025); coupled with petitRADTRANS for joint interior–atmosphere retrievals · 1 paper
- [[GENESIS]] — 1D self-consistent radiative-convective atmospheric model (Gandhi & Madhusudhan 2017, 2020) · 2 papers
- [[HELIOS]] — 1D radiative-transfer and thermal-structure code for exoplanet emission modeling · 3 papers
- [[HyDRo]] — atmospheric retrieval for hot rocky planet emission spectra (Gandhi & Madhusudhan 2018; Piette et al. 2022) · 1 paper
- [[NAMELESS]] — JWST NIRISS/SOSS time-series reduction pipeline (Montréal group); most conservative of three cross-checks in Lim 2023 · 2 papers
- [[Pegasus]] — JWST time-series reduction pipeline (Beatty et al. 2024); used as independent cross-check for MIRI/LRS · 1 paper
- [[Photochem]] — 1D photochemistry / chemical-kinetics code (Wogan et al. 2024); alternative to VULCAN, coupled with ScCHIMERA · 3 papers
- [[petitRADTRANS]] — open-source radiative-transfer and atmospheric retrieval code (Mollière et al. 2019); correlated-k and line-by-line modes for transmission/emission · 4 papers
- [[PICASO]] — radiative-transfer code for transmission and emission spectra · 11 papers
- [[POSEIDON]] (hub) — Bayesian atmospheric retrieval code for transmission and emission spectra · 9 papers
- [[ScCHIMERA]] — self-consistent radiative-convective + photochemistry CHIMERA variant (Piskorz et al. 2018; Iyer et al. 2023) · 1 paper
- [[SCARLET]] — frequentist exoplanet atmospheric retrieval and model-comparison code (Benneke & Seager 2012–2019); σ-significance rejection statistics · 5 papers
- [[smint]] — three-layer interior structure interpolator (Piaulet 2021–2022; Aguichine 2021 grid) for water-mass-fraction inference · 1 paper
- [[SOSSISSE]] — JWST NIRISS/SOSS reduction pipeline introduced in Lim 2023 (Montréal group) · 1 paper
- [[SPARTA]] — JWST time-series reduction pipeline, independent of the `jwst` pipeline · 7 papers
- [[SPHINX]] — Iyer 2023 cool-dwarf spectral grid; floor 2000 K · 3 papers
- [[spotrod]] — semi-analytic transit + occulted-spot light-curve model (Béky et al. 2014) · 3 papers
- [[Eureka]] (hub) — end-to-end JWST time-series reduction pipeline · 28 papers
- [[Iraclis]] — HST WFC3 spatial-scanning time-series reduction pipeline (Tsiaras et al. 2016); ARES survey standard · 1 paper
- [[JExoRES]] — JWST exoplanet reduction pipeline (Holmberg & Madhusudhan 2023); Hot Rocks Survey standard · 1 paper
- [[EXOFASTv2]] — MCMC code for simultaneous transit + RV system-parameter fitting · 2 papers
- [[ExoTiC-JEDI]] — end-to-end JWST time-series reduction pipeline · 9 papers
- [[exoTEDRF]] — Montréal-group JWST pipeline for NIRISS/SOSS and NIRSpec; standard for the NEAT GTO program · 7 papers
- [[FIREFLy]] (hub) — JWST time-series reduction pipeline with custom 1/f and bias handling · 8 papers
- [[Frida]] — Rathcke's custom JWST time-series pipeline (independent of `jwst` from stage 2) · 1 paper
- [[juliet]] — light-curve and RV fitting package (Espinoza 2019); supports GP detrending, nested sampling, MCMC · 8 papers
- [[llamaradas-estelares]] — empirical stellar-flare light-curve template package (Tovar Mendoza et al. 2022) · 1 paper
- [[speclib]] — Rackham 2023 Python interpolator for stellar-spectrum grids (notably SPHINX) · 1 paper
- [[spotter]] — Garcia & Rackham 2023 stellar-surface spot/chord visualization tool · 1 paper
- [[stctm]] — stellar contamination modeling code for M-dwarf PRISM hosts (Piaulet-Ghorayeb 2024; github.com/cpiaulet/stctm) · 1 paper
- [[supreme-SPOON]] — JWST NIRISS/SOSS time-series reduction pipeline (Feinstein, Coulombe, Radica et al.) · 3 papers
- [[TEATRO]] — alternate JWST NIRSpec time-series reduction pipeline (ExoMIRI consortium); choice vs. CASCADe shifts retrieved C/O on HAT-P-12 b · 2 papers
- [[TauREX3]] — open-source Bayesian atmospheric retrieval framework (Al-Refaie et al. 2019); ARES survey standard; supports transmission and emission · 4 papers
- [[MOPSMAP]] — Mie-scattering aerosol optical-property package (Gasteiger & Wiegner 2018) · 1 paper
- [[NEXOTRANS]] — hybrid Bayesian + machine-learning atmospheric retrieval framework (Deka et al. 2025); PyMultiNest + UltraNest + Random Forest/Gradient Boosting/kNN/Stacking Regressor; in-house **NEXOCHEM** equilibrium-chemistry module · 1 paper
- [[SANSAR]] — Suite of Adaptable plaNetary atmoSphere model And Retrieval (Verma et al. 2025); opacity-sampling and correlated-k retrieval; benchmarked against POSEIDON/ATMO/CHIMERA/Aurora · 1 paper
- [[NEMESISPY]] — Python implementation of the NEMESIS optimal-estimation retrieval framework (Yang et al. 2024) · 2 papers
- [[MultiNest]] — multimodal nested-sampling algorithm (Feroz & Hobson 2008); widely used retrieval sampler · 13 papers
- [[nestle]] — pure-Python nested sampler (Skilling; Barbary); chosen over emcee when KDE-form priors are required · 1 paper
- [[PACMAN-P]] — coupled atmosphere–interior geochemical evolution model (Krissansen-Totton et al. 2024); magma-ocean speciation + escape over Gyr · 1 paper
- [[PSG]] — Planetary Spectrum Generator (Villanueva et al. 2018; NASA GSFC); web-based radiative-transfer and retrieval tool · 1 paper
- [[PyratBay]] — Python radiative-transfer and atmospheric retrieval framework (Cubillos & Blecic 2021) · 1 paper
- [[RADYN]] — 1D non-LTE radiative-hydrodynamics stellar-atmosphere code (Carlsson & Stein 1995–1997); used for M-dwarf flare-template modeling · 1 paper
- [[Tiberius]] — JWST time-series reduction pipeline (extended from ground-based LRG-BEASTS) · 7 papers
- [[Tshirt]] — JWST time-series reduction pipeline (Schlawin & Glidic 2022); group-level 1/f handling preferred for NIRCam · 2 papers
- [[transitspectroscopy]] — Python JWST/NIRSpec reduction package (Espinoza 2022) · 5 papers
- [[VIRGA]] — eddy-diffusion-balanced cloud microphysics (Rooney et al. 2022); Ackerman & Marley framework · 1 paper
- [[VULCAN]] — 1D/2D photochemistry and chemical-kinetics code (Tsai et al. 2017, 2021, 2024) · 4 papers

## Programs (30)

- [[JWST-COM-2734]] — JWST commissioning 2734; NIRISS/SOSS transmission of HAT-P-18 b and WASP-96 b (Fu, Fournier-Tondreau, Radica, Taylor) · 2 papers
- [[JWST-ERS-1366]] — JWST Early Release Science program (PI: N. Batalha); multi-instrument characterization of WASP-39 b; first JWST CO₂ and SO₂ detections in an exoplanet atmosphere · 2 papers
- [[JWST-GO-2488]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2488 (PI: Sikora); 20.9-hr partial phase curve of highly eccentric hot Jupiter HD 80606 b at periapse with NIRSpec G395H · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-3838]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 3838 (PIs: Kirk & Ahrer); BOWIE-ALIGN comparative study of aligned vs misaligned hot Jupiters around F-stars · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-2722]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2722 (PI: Madhusudhan); NIRISS/SOSS + NIRSpec/G395H transits of K2-18 b designed to test the Hycean atmosphere hypothesis · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-4098]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 4098 (PIs: Benneke & Evans-Soma); NIRSpec G395H + NIRISS SOSS multi-instrument transit observations of TOI-270 d · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-4818]] — JWST Cycle 3 GO 4818 (PI: Weiner Mansfield); LAVA LAMPS survey of USP rocky planets with MIRI/LRS secondary eclipses · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-5924]] — JWST Cycle 3 GO 5924; NIRISS/SOSS transmission of WASP-80 b (Gascon, in prep) and WASP-94 Ab (Mukherjee et al. 2025) · 1 paper
- [[JWST-DD-9235]] — JWST Director's Discretionary Time DD 9235 (Espinoza & Diamond-Lowe); Rocky Worlds program; MIRI F1500W eclipse photometry of small rocky planets · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-1743]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 1743 (M. Mansfield) targeting Gl 486 b with MIRI/LRS emission · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-1981-CHAMPs]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 1981 (Stevenson & Lustig-Yaeger) surveying five rocky M-dwarf planets near the cosmic shoreline · 4 papers
- [[JWST-GO-2420]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2420 (Rathcke) targeting TRAPPIST-1 c with NIRSpec PRISM, including a back-to-back b+c visit · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-2512-COMPASS]] (hub) — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2512 (N. E. Batalha & Teske) surveying 11 small (1–3 R⊕) planets for population-level atmospheric characterization · 4 papers
- [[JWST-GO-2062]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2062 (PI: A. W. Mayo); NIRISS/SOSS transmission of WASP-166 b · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-2149]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2149 (PI: J.-M. Désert); NIRSpec G395H/SUB2048 transmission of the young (10–30 Myr) sub-Neptune progenitor V1298 Tau b · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-2589]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2589 (O. Lim); NIRISS/SOSS atmospheric reconnaissance of TRAPPIST-1 planets · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-2708]] — JWST Cycle 1 GO 2708 (Berta-Thompson) targeting LTT 1445 A b with MIRI/LRS emission · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-3315]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 3315 (PI: J. T. Wright); MIRI/LRS transit spectroscopy of the disintegrating rocky planet K2-22 b · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-3171]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 3171 (PIs: Kanodia, Cañas, Libby-Roberts); GEMS-JWST survey of seven gas giants around M dwarfs with NIRSpec PRISM · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-3730]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 3730 (PI: H. Diamond-Lowe); Hot Rocks Survey; MIRI F1500W eclipses of nine rocky M-dwarf planets · 2 papers
- [[JWST-GO-3860]] — JWST GO 3860 (PI: J. Teske); four NIRSpec G395H secondary eclipses of TOI-561 b; first detection consistent with thick atmosphere on a rocky planet · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-4082]] — JWST Cycle 2 GO 4082 (PI: M. Radica); single NIRSpec G395H transit of WASP-96 b · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GO-4195]] — JWST GO 4195 (PI: C. Fisher); five NIRSpec G395H transits of TOI-1685 b; flat spectrum; MMW ≳ 10 at ~5σ; bare rock · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GTO-1201]] — JWST Cycle 1 GTO 1201 (Lafrenière) NEAT survey; uses both NIRISS/SOSS and NIRSpec PRISM for rocky, sub-Neptune, and hot-Jupiter targets · 3 papers
- [[JWST-GTO-1224]] — JWST Cycle 1 GTO 1224 (Birkmann) targeting the L 98-59 system with NIRSpec · 2 papers
- [[JWST-GTO-1281]] — JWST Cycle 1 GTO 1281 (PIs: Lagage, Bouwman); ExoMIRI European Consortium panchromatic transmission spectroscopy of HAT-P-12 b · 1 paper
- [[JWST-GTO-1274]] — JWST Cycle 1 GTO 1274 (Lunine) targeting GJ 1132 b with MIRI/LRS secondary-eclipse spectroscopy · 1 paper
- [[JWST-TST-GTO-1312]] — JWST TST GTO 1312 (PI: N. Lewis); DREAMS Warm Neptune characterization of HAT-P-26 b with NIRSpec G395H + NIRISS/SOSS + MIRI/LRS · 1 paper
- [[JWST-TST-GTO-1331]] — JWST TST GTO 1331 (M. Mountain); DREAMS survey of TRAPPIST-1 planets with NIRSpec PRISM · 2 papers
- [[JWST-TST-GTO-1353]] — JWST TST GTO 1353 (PI: N. Lewis); DREAMS multi-instrument characterization of WASP-17 b · 3 papers

## Papers (61, newest first)

- [[2019_Rackham_TLS-FGK]] — Rackham, Apai, Giampapa 2019, AJ 157:96 — extends [[2018_Rackham_TLS]] to FGK dwarfs; reference activity-level spot fractions per spectral type (0.1 % F → 1.5–2.1 % K); demonstrates K-dwarf TLS produces visible blueward slopes at the magnitude observed for HD 189733 b; explicitly cites the McCullough 2014 reinterpretation; band-averaged molecular-feature contamination not detectable for typically active FGK dwarfs (exceptions: TiO/VO on active late-K, atomic line wings).
- [[2018_Rackham_TLS]] — Rackham, Apai, Giampapa 2018, ApJ 853:122 — foundational [[stellar-contamination]] (transit-light-source-effect) theory paper; rotating-photosphere forward models for M0V–M9V; A ∝ f_spot^0.5 calibration; M-dwarf TLS contamination is 1–15× the planetary atmospheric signal for rocky targets; biases TRAPPIST-1 densities by Δ(ρ) ≈ −8 %.
- [[2014_McCullough_HD189733b]] — McCullough, Crouzet, Deming, Madhusudhan 2014, ApJ 791:55 — single HST/WFC3 G141 spatial-scan transit of HD 189733 b; first H₂O detection (200 ± 47 ppm at 1.4 μm + 83 ± 53 ppm at 1.15 μm); reinterprets the [[2008_Pont_HD189733b]] / [[2013_Pont_HD189733b]] panchromatic Rayleigh slope as unocculted-starspot contamination + clear atmosphere; alkali condensation onto KCl/Na₂S explains missing Na/K wings.
- [[2013_Pont_HD189733b]] — Pont, Sing, Gibson, Aigrain, Henry, Husnoo 2013, MNRAS 432:2917 — panchromatic 0.3–24 μm transmission of HD 189733 b combining HST STIS+ACS+WFC3+NICMOS + Spitzer IRAC+MIPS; near-monotonic Rayleigh-like blueward radius increase across UV-to-NIR; narrow Na D + tentative K (2.5σ) cores only; dust-dominated atmosphere across ≥5 scale heights; antigreenhouse temperature inversion; pM/pL hot-Jupiter dichotomy reframed as clear-vs-dusty.
- [[2008_Pont_HD189733b]] — Pont, Knutson, Gilliland, Moutou, Charbonneau 2008, MNRAS 385:109 — three HST/ACS G800L transits of HD 189733 b (550–1050 nm); featureless visible spectrum + 472 km redward slope + 1000 km IR–visible offset; first conclusive aerosol claim in an exoplanet atmosphere.
- [[2025_Murphy_WASP107b]] — Murphy et al. 2025, AJ 170:61 — JWST 1–12 μm panchromatic limb-resolved analysis of WASP-107 b (NIRCam F322W2 + F444W, NIRISS/SOSS, NIRSpec G395H, MIRI/LRS); confirms ~180 K evening–morning T gradient and **resolves chemical heterogeneity** between limbs (5× SO₂, 40× CO₂, uniform H₂O, broad 6–11 μm cloud only on morning limb); develops [[Catwoman]] + `starry` injection-recovery correction for occulted-starspot bias on limb-resolved spectra
- [[2025_Verma_HD209458b]] — Verma, Goyal, Avarsekar, Shukla 2025, AJ 170:69 — introduces **SANSAR** atmospheric retrieval framework; benchmarks on WASP-96 b against POSEIDON/ATMO/CHIMERA/Aurora; applies to HD 209458 b across HST/STIS + HST/WFC3 + JWST/NIRCam combinations; demonstrates that NIRCam-only retrievals overestimate H₂O and CO₂ abundances by ~3 dex without optical baseline; full panchromatic retrieval gives solar metallicity + C/O ≈ 0.54 (consistent with stellar)
- [[2025_Barat_V1298Tau-b]] — Barat et al. 2025, AJ 170:165 — JWST/NIRSpec G395H + HST/WFC3 panchromatic transmission of the young (10–30 Myr) sub-Neptune progenitor V1298 Tau b; CO₂ at 35σ + H₂O at 30σ + CO at 10σ + CH₄ at 6σ + SO₂ at 4σ + tentative OCS; ~0.6× solar metallicity; subsolar C/O ≈ 0.22; CH₄ thermometer requires high T_int ≈ 500 K inconsistent with evolutionary models; **mass measurement (12 ± 1 M⊕) from atmospheric scale height** retires 200 M⊕ RV upper limit at 40σ
- [[2025_Deka_WASP39b]] — Deka et al. 2025, ApJ 989:50 — introduces **NEXOTRANS** Bayesian + machine-learning retrieval framework (PyMultiNest, UltraNest + Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, kNN, Stacking Regressor); built-in **NEXOCHEM** equilibrium chemistry module benchmarked vs. FastChem; modified hybrid equilibrium and modified equilibrium-offset chemistry frameworks; applied to WASP-39 b NIRISS + NIRSpec PRISM + MIRI panchromatic data; O/H = 14× solar, C/H = 21× solar, S/H = 5× solar, C/O = 1.35× solar; cross-validated against eight community retrieval codes
- [[2025_Fu_limb-asymmetry]] — Fu et al. 2025, ApJL 989:L17 — uniform NIRISS/SOSS reanalysis of all nine archival hot-Jupiter transmission datasets (T_eq = 770–1740 K); 3/9 (WASP-39 b, WASP-94 Ab, WASP-17 b) show > 5σ muted-morning / clear-evening 1.4 μm water-band contrast requiring high-altitude (0.1–0.01 mbar) aerosols on the morning limb cleared on day timescales; introduces the Limb Spectroscopy Metric and "asymmetry horizon" empirical boundary in (T_eq, log g) space; quantifies up to +2 dex metallicity / ½× temperature retrieval bias from unrecognized limb asymmetry
- [[2025_Coulombe_TOI-270b]] — Coulombe et al. 2025, AJ 170:226 — JWST/NIRSpec G395H simultaneous transits of warm super-Earth TOI-270 b and the sub-Neptune TOI-270 d; updated bulk density 3.7 ± 0.5 g cm⁻³ → 4.4σ underdense vs. Earth-like, requires WMF ~ 5–6%; tentative H₂O absorption feature near 3 μm at lnB = 0.3–3.2 (offset-dependent); methodological first — uses simultaneous TOI-270 d transit (3× more TLS-sensitive) to rule out stellar contamination; PACMAN-P shows long-lived secondary atmosphere is plausible despite cosmic-shoreline placement
- [[2025_Sikora_HD80606b]] — Sikora et al. 2025, AJ 170:105 — JWST NIRSpec G395H 20.9-hour partial phase curve of highly eccentric hot Jupiter HD 80606 b (e=0.93); CH₄ at 4.1–10.7σ + H₂O at 4.2–5.5σ + CO₂ at 3.7–4.4σ post-periapse; T_bright 892 → 1313 → 1077 K through periapse; **rules out strong temperature inversion** despite GCM predictions; chemical-equilibrium retrievals preferred
- [[2025_Jaziri_K2-18b]] — Jaziri & Drant 2025, A&A 702:L20 — investigates photochemical (tholin) hazes as a way to reconcile K2-18 b NIRISS+NIRSpec G395H vs MIRI/LRS amplitude tension; CH₄-only PAMPRE "exo1" tholin haze adds 7 μm C–H bending + scattering shorter-wavelength flattening; included at Bayes factor 5.53; reduces inferred CH₄ by ~2 dex and metallicity from 20× to 0.3× solar; reinforces metallicity-aerosol-composition degeneracy
- [[2025_Crossfield_SO2shoreline]] — Crossfield, Ahrer, Brande, Kreidberg, Lothringer, Piaulet-Ghorayeb, Polman, Welbanks, Kirk, Powell, Khorshid 2025, AJ submitted (arXiv:2509.14318) — theoretical 936-model HELIOS+VULCAN+petitRADTRANS grid mapping the SO₂ shoreline in (T_eq, [M/H], C/O, K_zz, T_int, XUV) space; SO₂ depends strongly on metallicity and C/O, weakly on XUV/K_zz/T_int; SO₂ never the dominant atmospheric sulfur species but most easily detectable
- [[2025_Madhusudhan_subneptunes]] — Madhusudhan, Holmberg, Constantinou, Cooke 2025, PNAS — perspective/review on JWST sub-Neptune characterization; introduces a 2-axis (T_eq × H₂ content) classification scheme yielding 6 categories (ice / hycean / mini-Neptune / gas dwarf / supercritical mini-Neptune / steam world); T_crit ≳ 350 K; reviews K2-18 b, TOI-270 d, GJ 9827 d as cornerstone targets; biosignature candidates DMS/CS₂/OCS/CH₃Cl/N₂O
- [[2025_FernandezRodriguez_K2-18b]] — Fernández-Rodríguez et al. 2025, A&A submitted (arXiv:2510.18098) — independent reanalysis of JWST GO 2722 NIRISS SOSS + NIRSpec G395H transits of K2-18 b; CH₄ robust at 4.16σ–4.62σ across 12 spectrum variants; CO₂ marginal at ~2σ in comprehensive retrievals; **DMS retracted** (Bayes factor < 1); supersolar C/O ≈ 10–13 supports Inside-Out Planet Formation
- [[2025_Felix_TOI-270d]] — Felix et al. 2025, A&A 701:A296 — independent reanalysis of JWST GO 4098 NIRSpec G395H + NIRISS SOSS transits of temperate sub-Neptune TOI-270 d using Eureka! + BeAR; reproduces Benneke 2024 metal-rich result (MMW = 5.56 amu, [M/H] ~160× solar) but cannot reproduce Holmberg & Madhusudhan 2024 hycean fit; CH₄ + CO₂ decisive; tentative CS₂ degenerate with CH₃Cl/CH₃F/H₂CS; SO₂ retracted; native-resolution + LSF retrieval argued essential
- [[2025_Ahrer_KELT7b]] — Ahrer et al. 2025, MNRAS 543:2442 — JWST NIRSpec G395H transmission of aligned hot Jupiter KELT-7 b under BOWIE-ALIGN (GO 3838); weak/tentative H₂O and CO₂ only; two scenarios (high cloud deck OR low-metallicity atmosphere); equilibrium chemistry preferred at Δ ln Z = 1.6–3.1
- [[2025_Gressier_HATP26b]] — Gressier et al. 2025, AJ 170:292 — JWST-TST DREAMS NIRSpec G395H transmission of warm Neptune-mass HAT-P-26 b; SO₂ at lnB = 13.46 (first SO₂ in a Neptune-mass planet), CO₂ at lnB = 85.64, H₂O at lnB = 4.06; ~10× solar metallicity, sub-solar C/O = 0.14; population reanalysis of 11 JWST giants validates Crossfield SO₂ shoreline
- [[2026_Coy_HD3167b]] — Coy et al. 2026, ApJ — JWST MIRI/LRS secondary eclipse of USP super-Earth HD 3167 b (LAVA LAMPS); eclipse 38±11 ppm vs. 104±3 ppm bare-rock; R=0.57; ~5σ atmospheric evidence; first USP super-Earth with such evidence
- [[2025_Crouzet_HATP12b]] — Crouzet et al. 2025, A&A 703:A264 — foundational NIRSpec G395M paper for HAT-P-12 b; CO₂ at 12.2σ, H₂O at 6.0σ, CO at 4.1σ; metallicity 10× solar with photochemistry, ~60× without
- [[2025_Howard_TRAPPIST1_flares]] — Howard et al. 2025, arXiv:2512.04265 — RADYN flare-modeling pipeline for JWST NIR transmission of TRAPPIST-1 b/c/f/g; residual noise 54–65 ppm; first wiki characterization of TRAPPIST-1 f and g
- [[2025_AcunaAguirre_WASP80b]] — Acuña-Aguirre et al. 2025, A&A (arXiv:2511.13483) — joint interior–atmosphere retrievals (GASTLI + petitRADTRANS) on WASP-80 b panchromatic HST + JWST data; two fiducial scenarios bracketing Z_p = 0.12–0.28 and M/H = 2.75–10× solar
- [[2025_Roy_LP791-18c]] — Roy et al. 2025, Nature Astronomy — JWST NIRSpec PRISM transmission of the temperate sub-Neptune LP 791-18 c; strong haze (5.4σ) + CH₄ (4.5σ) but **no CO₂**; sub-Neptune diversity contrast with K2-18 b and TOI-270 d
- [[2025_Gordon_COMPASS-G395H]] — Gordon et al. 2025, arXiv:2511.18196 — uniform ExoTiC-JEDI reanalysis of seven COMPASS NIRSpec G395H spectra; introduces RPF-PCA systematics model; quantifies NRS1/NRS2 detector offsets and PandExo under-prediction
- [[2026_Radica_WASP96b]] — Radica et al. 2026, arXiv:2604.05049 — new JWST NIRSpec G395H + archival NIRISS/SOSS + VLT/FORS2 panchromatic transit of WASP-96 b; H₂O, CO₂, Na detected; tentative SO₂ at lnB=2.69; super-stellar metallicity; on the proposed SO₂ photochemical shoreline
- [[2026_Holmberg_GJ3473b]] — Holmberg et al. 2026, arXiv:2604.02332 — Hot Rocks Survey V; four MIRI F1500W eclipses of GJ 3473 b; joint depth 186±45 ppm, R=0.82±0.12; bare-rock and atmosphere both consistent; thick CO₂ excluded above 1.2–6.5 bar
- [[2026_Heinke_HATP12b]] — Heinke et al. 2026, A&A (arXiv:2604.01219) — panchromatic 0.36–12.4 μm JWST+HST transit of warm sub-Saturn HAT-P-12 b; H₂O, CO₂, CO, H₂S detected; information-content study attributes detections to specific instrument combinations
- [[2026_Ashtari_HATS75b]] — Ashtari et al. 2026, AJ accepted (arXiv:2604.07268) — three NIRSpec PRISM transits of HATS-75 b; first wiki paper on a Giant Exoplanet around an M-dwarf Star (GEMS); CH₄, CO, CO₂ detected; sub-solar atmospheric metallicity in TLS framework
- [[2025_Connors_MIRI-15um]] — Connors et al. 2025, ApJL 989:L11 — uniform FN-PCA reanalysis of 17 MIRI F1500W eclipses across 5 rocky planets (TRAPPIST-1 b/c, LHS 1478 b, TOI-1468 b, LHS 1140 c); introduces Erebus pipeline; TRAPPIST-1 c dayside cooler than Zieba 2023
- [[2025_LustigYaeger_WASP17b]] — Lustig-Yaeger et al. 2025, arXiv:2510.06169 — JWST-TST DREAMS NIRSpec G395H transit + secondary eclipse of WASP-17 b; introduces iESR-PIE for first JWST nightside emission spectrum; tentative SO₂, CH₄, H₂O, CO₂ on nightside; A_B = 0.42
- [[2025_Gressier_WASP17b]] — Gressier et al. 2025, AJ 169:57 — JWST-TST DREAMS NIRISS/SOSS dayside secondary eclipse of WASP-17 b; H₂O at 6.4σ supersolar; non-inverted T–P; FeH tentative at 3σ
- [[2026_RoyPerez_WASP39b]] — Roy-Perez et al. 2026, A&A 707:A297 — data-reduction systematics study on WASP-39 b NIRSpec PRISM ERS data; four pipelines, six reductions; retrieved abundances vary by up to 2 dex; Na, K, H₂O, CO₂, CO, SO₂ confirmed; H₂S tentative
- [[2025_Tusay_K2-22b]] — Tusay et al. 2025, ApJL 987:L6 — four JWST MIRI/LRS transit windows of the disintegrating rocky planet K2-22 b; one 9.7σ detection; iron-rich silicate dust; tentative NO gas at ~5.1 μm; GO 3315
- [[2021_Mugnai_GJ1132b]] — Mugnai et al. 2021, AJ 161:284 — five HST WFC3 G141 transits of GJ 1132 b (ARES V); flat 1.1–1.6 μm; no molecular absorption; rebuts Swain et al. 2021 HCN/H₂ claim
- [[2025_Teske_TOI561b]] — Teske et al. 2025, ApJL 995:L39 — four NIRSpec G395H secondary eclipses of TOI-561 b; T_day = 1800–2150 K vs. bare-rock ~2950 K; first detection consistent with thick atmosphere on a dense rocky exoplanet; challenges cosmic shoreline
- [[2025_PiauletGhorayeb_TRAPPIST1d]] — Piaulet-Ghorayeb et al. 2025, ApJ 989:181 — two NIRSpec PRISM transits of TRAPPIST-1 d; flat ±100–150 ppm; H₂ excluded >3σ; cloud-free Venus/early Mars/modern Earth excluded >95%; N₂-rich consistent
- [[2025_Fisher_TOI1685b]] — Fisher et al. 2025, MNRAS (arXiv:2512.15338) — five NIRSpec G395H transits of TOI-1685 b; flat; MMW ≳ 10 at ~5σ; CO₂, SO₂, H₂O pure atmospheres ruled out; bare rock
- [[2025_Espinoza_TRAPPIST1e]] — Espinoza et al. 2025, ApJL 990:L52 — four NIRSpec PRISM transits of TRAPPIST-1 e; novel GP marginalization over stellar contamination; PHOENIX models fail for TRAPPIST-1; cloudy H₂-dominated atmospheres ruled out >3σ
- [[2025_Xue_GJ3929b]] — Xue et al. 2025, ApJL 995:L52 — two MIRI F1500W photometric eclipses of GJ 3929 b; T_day = 782 ± 79 K, R ≈ 1.07; bare-rock; CO₂ > 100 mbar excluded; new MAROON-X RVs refine mass to 1.13 ± 0.09 M⊕
- [[2025_Glidden_TRAPPIST1e]] — Glidden et al. 2025, ApJL 990:L53 — four JWST NIRSpec PRISM transits of TRAPPIST-1 e; featureless; H₂-rich (5σ) and thick CO₂ (6.9σ) ruled out; μ > 8.6 u; N₂-rich atmosphere consistent; stellar models dominate systematics
- [[2025_Redai_GJ357b]] — Redai et al. 2025, arXiv:2507.07165 — JWST COMPASS NIRSpec G395H transmission spectrum of GJ 357 b; featureless 2.87–5.14 μm; rules out MMW < 8 g/mol and metallicity < 300–500× solar
- [[2025_Taylor_GJ357b]] — Taylor et al. 2025, MNRAS — first JWST NIRISS/SOSS transmission spectrum of GJ 357 b; featureless; rules out ≤100× solar metallicity with cloud-top pressure > 0.01 bar at 3σ; strong case for secondary-atmosphere retention given exceptional host-star inactivity
- [[2025_Teske_TOI776c]] — Teske & Batalha et al. 2025, AJ 169:249 — JWST COMPASS two-visit NIRSpec G395H of the sub-Neptune TOI-776 c; featureless 3–5 μm; rules out metallicity < 180–240× solar; MMW limit model dependent (2.8–7.7 g mol⁻¹)
- [[2025_Rathcke_TRAPPIST1bc]] — Rathcke et al. 2025, ApJL — back-to-back NIRSpec PRISM transits of TRAPPIST-1 b and c; proof of concept for empirical stellar-contamination correction; 2.5× red-noise reduction at 0.8–2 μm; well-mixed photosphere of ~2000 K + ~2600 K components
- [[2025_Bennett_GJ1132b]] — Bennett et al. 2025, AJ — four-visit NIRSpec G395H + G395M coadded spectrum of GJ 1132 b is flat; retires the 2023 tentative H₂O+CH₄ inference; concludes the planet is a bare rock
- [[2025_Alam_L168-9b]] — Alam et al. 2025, AJ — first broadband 3–12 μm (NIRSpec + MIRI) transmission spectrum of a rocky exoplanet (L 168-9 b); featureless; rules out <100× solar metallicity
- [[2025_Alderson_TOI-776b]] — Alderson et al. 2025, arXiv:2501.14596 — JWST COMPASS NIRSpec G395H on super-Earth TOI-776 b; flat after detector-offset correction; rules out <100× solar
- [[2024_WeinerMansfield_GJ486b]] — Weiner Mansfield et al. 2024, ApJL — two MIRI/LRS secondary eclipses of Gl 486 b; T_day=865 K, R=0.97; rules out Earth-/Venus-like atmospheres; retires the water-rich transmission interpretation from Moran 2023
- [[2024_Wachiraphan_LTT1445Ab]] — Wachiraphan et al. 2024, arXiv:2410.10987 — three MIRI/LRS secondary eclipses of LTT 1445 A b; T_day=525 K; rules out thick CO₂ atmospheres at >6σ
- [[2024_Xue_GJ1132b]] — Xue et al. 2024, ApJL 973:L8 — JWST GTO 1274 MIRI/LRS secondary eclipse of GJ 1132 b; T_day=709±31 K, R=0.95±0.04; bare-rock consistent; rules out Earth-like and Venus-like atmospheres
- [[2024_Scarsdale_L98-59c]] — Scarsdale et al. 2024, AJ — JWST COMPASS two-visit NIRSpec G395H transmission spectrum of L 98-59 c; featureless; rules out <300× solar metallicity
- [[2024_Gressier_L98-59d]] — Gressier et al. 2024, ApJL — single-visit NIRSpec G395H transmission spectrum of L 98-59 d; sulfur-rich atmosphere preferred at 2.6σ–5.6σ (companion "Paper I")
- [[2024_Banerjee_L98-59d]] — Banerjee et al. 2024, ApJL — companion "Paper II" retrievals on the L 98-59 d transit; preferred scenario has unocculted faculae + H₂S/SO₂ atmosphere
- [[2024_Alderson_TOI-836b]] — Alderson et al. 2024, AJ — JWST COMPASS NIRSpec G395H on super-Earth TOI-836 b; flat spectrum rules out H₂-dominated atmospheres at <250× solar
- [[2023_May_GJ1132b]] — May et al. 2023, ApJL — two JWST NIRSpec G395H transits of GJ 1132 b yield discrepant spectra; a cautionary tale and ruling-out of the HST-era HCN / H₂ atmosphere
- [[2023_Moran_GJ486b]] — Moran et al. 2023, ApJL — two NIRSpec G395H transits of GJ 486 b; water-rich atmosphere OR unocculted starspots fit equally well (later resolved in favor of starspots by Weiner Mansfield 2024)
- [[2023_Lustig-Yaeger_LHS475b]] — Lustig-Yaeger et al. 2023, Nature Astronomy — first JWST transmission spectrum of an Earth-sized exoplanet (LHS 475 b); featureless; demonstrates no NIRSpec noise floor above 5 ppm
- [[2023_Lim_TRAPPIST1b]] — Lim et al. 2023, ApJL 955:L22 — two NIRISS/SOSS transits of TRAPPIST-1 b; stellar contamination dominates (spots Visit 1, faculae Visit 2); H₂-rich atmospheres rejected at 16–29σ; secondary atmospheres unconstrained
- [[2001_Brown_TransmissionDiagnostics]] — Brown 2001, ApJ 553:1006 — foundational transmission-spectroscopy theory; introduces the spectrum-ratio R(λ) framework, parameterized cloud decks, Doppler / wind / rotation diagnostics, Na/K UV photoionization on HD 209458 b
- [[2000_Seager_TheoryTransmission]] — Seager & Sasselov 2000, ApJ 537:916 — **the** foundational transmission-spectroscopy paper; first quantitative predictions for HD 209458 b; Na D doublet identified as the strongest expected feature; motivated the historic Charbonneau et al. 2002 first-detection (not yet ingested)

## Analyses (2, newest first)

- [[analyses/2026-05-03_terrestrial-planets-state-of-the-field]] — synthesis across 24 terrestrial planets and ~25 papers; catalogues bare-rock-confirmed / featureless / inconclusive / tentative-positive results; documents the four-strategy stellar-contamination toolkit; foregrounds the TOI-561 b / HD 3167 b / L 98-59 d challengers to the cosmic shoreline.
- [[analyses/2026-04-29_rocky-planets-jwst-transit-and-eclipse]] — four rocky planets (GJ 1132 b, GJ 486 b, TRAPPIST-1 b, TRAPPIST-1 c) have both JWST transit and JWST eclipse coverage; per-target details, the GJ 486 b transmission-degeneracy resolution by emission, and a systematics catalog (NRS2 superbias, photosphere geometry, FN-PCA quirks).
